chapter: Chapter: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Why were Indian textiles famous in the world before British rule?
questions.answer: They were known for their fine quality, craftsmanship and unique designs.

questions.question: Why did the demand for Indian textiles decline in the nineteenth century?
questions.answer: Machine-made textiles from Britain flooded Indian markets.

questions.question: How did the Industrial Revolution affect Indian weavers?
questions.answer: It created competition from cheap factory-made cloth.

questions.question: Why did British policies favour British textile industries?
questions.answer: They imposed duties on Indian textiles and promoted British imports.

questions.question: Why did many Indian weavers lose their livelihoods?
questions.answer: Demand for handwoven cloth decreased due to factory-made textiles.

questions.question: Why did some Indian weavers switch to agriculture?
questions.answer: Because weaving no longer provided sufficient income.

questions.question: Why were Indian handloom products unable to compete with British cloth?
questions.answer: British cloth was produced faster and cheaper in factories.

questions.question: How did colonial policies affect Indian handicraft industries?
questions.answer: They led to the decline of traditional crafts.

questions.question: Why did British manufacturers prefer machine production?
questions.answer: It increased production and reduced costs.

questions.question: Why did some Indian weavers migrate to cities during the nineteenth century?
questions.answer: They searched for alternative employment.

questions.question: Why were Indian iron smelters affected by colonial policies?
questions.answer: Forest laws restricted access to wood needed for charcoal.

questions.question: Why did iron smelting decline in India during the nineteenth century?
questions.answer: Modern iron factories replaced traditional smelting methods.

questions.question: Why did the British import iron from Britain instead of encouraging Indian production?
questions.answer: They wanted to support British industries.

questions.question: Why were forests important for traditional iron smelters?
questions.answer: They provided wood used to make charcoal.

questions.question: Why did colonial forest laws affect tribal communities?
questions.answer: They restricted their traditional use of forest resources.

questions.question: Why did factory production increase during the nineteenth century?
questions.answer: Industrial machines improved speed and efficiency.

questions.question: Why did British factories produce cloth in large quantities?
questions.answer: Machines allowed mass production.

questions.question: Why were Indian markets important for British manufacturers?
questions.answer: They provided a large market for British goods.

questions.question: Why did traditional weaving decline during British rule?
questions.answer: Because of industrial competition and colonial policies.

questions.question: Why were factory owners able to dominate the textile market?
questions.answer: They used machines to produce cheaper goods.

questions.question: Why did British policies discourage Indian industrial development?
questions.answer: They aimed to protect British economic interests.

questions.question: Why were Indian craftsmen affected by industrialization?
questions.answer: Traditional handmade products lost demand.

questions.question: Why did industrialization change the nature of work?
questions.answer: Factory systems replaced traditional craft production.

questions.question: Why did Indian artisans struggle during colonial rule?
questions.answer: They faced competition from machine-made imports.

questions.question: Why is the study of weavers and iron smelters important in history?
questions.answer: It explains the economic impact of colonialism and industrialization.
